━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📚 DAILY MARKET EDUCATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🎯 TECHNICAL SPOTLIGHT: Reading Momentum Like a Pro

Momentum tells you WHO is in control — bulls or bears.

Think of momentum as the market's heartbeat. When price moves fast with conviction, momentum is strong. When it crawls sideways, momentum dies.

The RSI (Relative Strength Index) measures this on a 0-100 scale:
- Above 70 = overbought territory, watch for exhaustion
- Below 30 = oversold zone, potential bounce ahead
- Between 40-60 = neutral, no clear edge

Real example: NVIDIA hit RSI 78 in May 2026 after the AI chip rally. Smart traders took partial profits. Two weeks later, 12% pullback.

MACD is your momentum confirmation tool. When the MACD line crosses above the signal line, buyers are gaining strength. Below it? Sellers dominate.

Practical application:
- Don't chase stocks with RSI above 75
- Look for bullish MACD crossovers on pullbacks
- Combine with volume — momentum without volume is fake

PRO TIP: Divergence is gold. When price makes new highs but RSI doesn't, momentum is weakening. That's your early warning signal.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💵 FUNDAMENTAL FOCUS: Interest Rates & The Fed's Power

The Federal Reserve controls the cost of money in the economy.

When the Fed raises rates, borrowing becomes expensive. Companies slow expansion. Consumers spend less. Stock valuations compress, especially growth stocks.

When the Fed cuts rates, cheap money floods the system. Businesses invest. Stocks rally. Risk appetite returns.

Current situation June 2026:
Fed is holding rates at 4.50% after three cuts from the 5.50% peak. Chair Powell signaled "patience" in the last meeting, meaning rates stay steady for now.

Why this matters for YOUR trades:
- Tech stocks love low rates (cheaper to fund growth)
- Banks profit from higher rates (wider lending margins)
- Real estate is rate-sensitive (mortgage costs impact demand)

Watch these Fed indicators:
- Core PCE inflation target is 2%
- Unemployment rate threshold around 4%
- Any move outside these triggers Fed action

The Fed doesn't care about your portfolio. They care about price stability and employment. Trade accordingly.

SMART MONEY RULE: Fed meeting weeks bring volatility. Reduce position sizes 48 hours before announcements.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⚡ THREE TIPS FOR TODAY'S MARKET

TIP 1 — Watch the 10-Year Treasury Yield
Currently at 4.15%, this is your market compass. Rising yields pressure stocks. Falling yields support risk assets. Check it every morning before the open.

TIP 2 — Trade the First Hour, Then Wait
Maximum volatility hits 9:30-10:30 AM EST. Most retail traders lose money chasing this chaos. Let the dust settle. Better setups appear after 11 AM when institutions show their hand.

TIP 3 — Use Alerts, Not Screen Time
Set price alerts at key support and resistance levels. Stop staring at charts all day. The market will notify you when opportunity arrives. Preserve your mental capital.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🛡️ RISK MANAGEMENT: Controlling Portfolio Exposure

Position sizing separates professionals from gamblers.

Your total portfolio exposure is the percentage of capital at risk across all open positions. Many traders blow up because they don't track this number.

The 2% Rule:
Never risk more than 2% of your account on a single trade. On a 50,000 account, that's 1,000 max risk per position.

Calculate it properly:
Risk per trade = Entry price minus stop loss, multiplied by share quantity

Portfolio heat management:
- Total exposure across all trades should not exceed 6-8%
- If you have 4 positions, each risking 2%, you're at 8% total heat
- One bad day could cost you 8% — is that acceptable?

Correlation kills accounts. Don't open five tech positions thinking you're diversified. When NASDAQ dumps, all five bleed together.

Real scenario: Trader opens long positions in AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, META. Thinks he has five trades. He actually has ONE trade — long big tech. Sector rotation crushes him.

Diversify by sector, timeframe, and strategy type.

PROTECTIVE RULE: When portfolio heat exceeds 10%, stop opening new positions until something closes. Discipline beats conviction.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Educational purpose only
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
